message:
fix(unicode): implement correct backspace semantics for wide and combining text

Replace simple cursor.move_left(1) with apply_backspace() that correctly targets
the leading cell of wide graphemes (CJK characters) and preserves combining mark
integrity during editing operations:

- Skip wide character continuation cells to land on leading cell
- Handle pending_wrap state correctly for wide chars at line boundary
- Add 3 unit tests: wide grapheme backspace, pending wrap on wide char,
  combining cluster overwrite
- Add 2 differential test fixtures for regression coverage
- Update beads issue tracker (bd-2vr05.6.3 assignment)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.6","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): unicode width policy and grapheme correctness","description":"Background
- Unicode correctness is a standards and trust issue; errors here break real user text.
- Width ambiguity, grapheme clustering, and erase semantics are common sources of subtle bugs.

Goal
- Establish explicit unicode policy controls and correct runtime behavior across rendering/edit/search paths.

In Scope
- Width policy configuration and presets.
- Grapheme cluster handling for ZWJ/combining marks.
- Correct erase/backspace/cursor semantics.
- Search normalization aligned with policy settings.
- Conformance fixture corpus and CI gate.

Out of Scope
- Silent behavior changes without policy transparency.

Why This Matters
- Required for standards compliance and international usability.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unicode-heavy fixtures pass deterministically.
- Policies are explicit and reflected in behavior.
- Regressions are caught by conformance suite.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.655128351Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.418785231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","frankenterm","standards","unicode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.655128351Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.7","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): accessibility, focus model, and IME composition robustness","description":"Background
- Accessibility and IME are required for inclusive and global terminal usage.
- Canvas/WebGPU terminals can easily regress if these are bolted on late.

Goal
- Make accessibility and IME first-class, deterministic behaviors in FrankenTermJS.

In Scope
- Composition pipeline for IME preedit/commit flows.
- Focus model and keyboard-only navigation guarantees.
- Screen-reader mirror and live announcement policy.
- Reduced-motion and high-contrast integration.
- Cross-browser E2E accessibility matrix.

Out of Scope
- Minimal checkbox-only a11y claims without test evidence.

Why This Matters
- Necessary for real-world adoption and accessibility obligations.

Acceptance Criteria
- IME and keyboard-only paths function reliably.
- Screen-reader mirror behavior is coherent and bounded.
- Browser matrix tests provide reproducible evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.160403481Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.419218131Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","frankenterm","ime","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.160403481Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:04.736000514Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-fbp","title":"Implement inline mode overlay redraw baseline","description":"PHASE 2.5 - INLINE MODE IMPLEMENTATION (CRITICAL)

## Background
Inline mode is a NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement. The overlay redraw baseline is the correctness foundation that works everywhere, before optimization.

## Technical Requirements (from Plan Section D)

### Overlay Redraw Strategy
1. Save cursor position (DECSC or manual tracking)
2. Move cursor to UI region start
3. Clear UI region only (never full screen!)
4. Render UI frame
5. Restore cursor position (DECRC)

### UI Region Anchoring
- Bottom-anchored (default): UI at bottom, logs scroll above
- Top-anchored (optional): UI at top, logs scroll below
- Configurable height: ui_height parameter

### Log Write Coordination
write_log() must:
1. Suspend UI temporarily
2. Print log line to scrollback region
3. Re-render UI in its anchored position
4. Maintain cursor contract

### Cursor Contract
After any present():
- Cursor is at the correct position per active policy
- UI region is intact
- Scrollback is preserved

### Never Clear Full Screen
The DECRC/ED2 pattern that clears full screen is FORBIDDEN in inline mode.
Only clear the bounded UI region using EL (erase line) or localized ED.

## Implementation

pub struct InlinePresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    anchor: Anchor,  // Bottom or Top
    cursor_saved: bool,
}

impl InlinePresenter {
    pub fn present(&mut self, buffer: &Buffer, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        // 1. Save cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b7")?;  // DECSC
        
        // 2. Move to UI region
        let (_, term_height) = writer.size();
        let ui_start = match self.anchor {
            Anchor::Bottom => term_height - self.ui_height,
            Anchor::Top => 0,
        };
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        
        // 3. Clear UI region only (line by line)
        for row in 0..self.ui_height {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[2K")?;  // EL - clear line
            if row < self.ui_height - 1 {
                writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
            }
        }
        
        // 4. Move back and render
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        // ... emit diff ...
        
        // 5. Restore cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b8")?;  // DECRC
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] UI renders at correct position (bottom/top)
- [ ] Scrollback is preserved after present()
- [ ] Cursor returns to correct position
- [ ] Log writes don't corrupt UI
- [ ] Resize re-anchors correctly
- [ ] PTY test: verify no scrollback corruption

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlay redraw works on all terminals
- [ ] Cursor contract maintained
- [ ] UI region bounded, never full-screen clear
- [ ] Log coordination implemented
- [ ] PTY tests pass

## Performance Budget
- Overlay redraw: < 2ms (acceptable for correctness baseline)

 {"id":"bd-fp38v","title":"epic(alien-artifact): Unified Evidence Ledger Schema Across All Decision Points","description":"Unify ALL Bayesian decision points (diff strategy, resize coalescing, frame budget, degradation, VOI, hint ranking, command palette scoring) under a single structured Evidence Ledger schema. Every decision emits: log_posterior, top-3 evidence terms with Bayes factors, action chosen, loss avoided, confidence interval. EV=10.0 (cross-project). Enables galaxy-brain transparency, postmortems, and guardrail automation.

**User Value:** Every Bayesian decision is fully auditable via structured evidence logs, enabling developers to understand and debug adaptive behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Unified EvidenceLedger struct covers all 7 decision domains
- JSONL output with decision_id, evidence_terms, posterior, action
- All Bayesian controllers emit evidence on every decision
- Evidence replay produces identical decisions
- All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:11.520508236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:18:17.608200473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:18:17.608136213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","evidence","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v","depends_on_id":"bd-nv2fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:34.895615197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-fp38v.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Evidence Ledger","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Evidence Ledger (EV=10)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering with evidence emission enabled across all 7 Bayesian decision points. Verifies schema compliance, no missing fields, and correct evidence structure for every decision.

### Test Scenario
1. Enable evidence ledger emission for all 7 decision points:
   a. Diff strategy selection (incremental vs full)
   b. Resize coalescing (coalesce vs immediate)
   c. Frame budget management (admit vs defer)
   d. Degradation level selection (normal/reduced/minimal)
   e. VOI (value of information) for measurement decisions
   f. Hint ranking for command palette
   g. Command palette scoring
2. Render demo screens that exercise each decision point.
3. For diff strategy: alternate between small edits (incremental) and full redraws.
4. For resize: send rapid resize events.
5. For frame budget: inject artificial load spikes.
6. For degradation: gradually increase frame times.
7. Collect all JSONL evidence entries and validate schema.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each decision point emits a unified evidence entry:
```json
{
  "event": "evidence_ledger",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|frame_budget|degradation|voi|hint_rank|cmd_score",
  "log_posterior": f64,
  "top_evidence": [
    { "term": "string", "bayes_factor": f64, "direction": "for|against" }
  ],
  "action_chosen": "string",
  "loss_avoided": f64,
  "confidence_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64, "level": f64 },
  "alternatives_considered": u32,
  "frame_id": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points emit at least one evidence entry during the test run.
- Every evidence entry has all required fields (no nulls in required fields).
- top_evidence array has exactly 3 entries per decision (top-3 evidence terms).
- bayes_factor values are positive and finite (no NaN/Inf).
- confidence_interval.lower < confidence_interval.upper.
- Schema validates against JSON Schema definition.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
